projects.title: BibtexScraping
description: A robust automation tool designed to streamline literature review workflows by batch-fetching BibTeX citations from raw paper titles.

Users simply upload an Excel file containing paper titles, and the system intelligently searches Google Scholar via SerpApi. To ensure accuracy, it implements cross-validation of titles and falls back on Crossref and arXiv DOI inference, significantly reducing the risk of citing the wrong paper.

Built with Node.js and featuring a clean web UI, it handles API rate-limits gracefully with built-in retries and outputs a structured .xlsx file containing all the matched BibTeX entries and metadata.

projects.title: CXR-DR: Chest X-ray Diagnosis and Report Generation
description: A comprehensive deep learning framework designed for automated chest X-ray diagnosis and medical report generation, developed as my capstone thesis.

The system tackles critical challenges in medical imaging, specifically long-tailed distributions of rare pathologies and domain shifts across different X-ray scanners. It employs specialized ensemble CNNs with attention-guided feature learning to accurately identify subtle abnormalities.

To ensure cross-dataset robustness, it integrates adversarial scanner/domain debiasing techniques and leverages PubMedCLIP-based input validation. The AI engine is served via a high-performance FastAPI backend and consumed by a modern Next.js web application.

projects.title: LiteratureWikiGraphRAG
description: An innovative LLM-powered Wiki-style GraphRAG system designed specifically for academic literature review.

Unlike traditional approaches that treat papers as individual nodes, this system builds a sparse, semantic concept graph where nodes represent key methodologies, datasets, metrics, and research gaps. The actual papers serve as supporting evidence attached to these nodes and edges.

Developed with Python, Streamlit, and the Gemini API, it features an intelligent narrowing workflow that helps researchers distill broad topics down to a highly relevant core set of papers before extracting concepts and generating deeply cited, evidence-backed literature review drafts.
